name: Bitstamp Cryptocurrency Exchange API
what_it_does: Regulated European cryptocurrency exchange REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and institutions to access real-time market data, execute spot trades (BTC, ETH, XRP, LTC, BCH, and 50+ pairs), manage orders, access account balances, and handle withdrawals, with both REST API for trading operations and WebSocket API for real-time order book and ticker data.
use_cases: Executing algorithmic cryptocurrency trades via Bitstamp REST API for automated trading strategies
Streaming real-time order book data via Bitstamp WebSocket API for market analysis tools
Managing crypto portfolio via Bitstamp API for institutional account management
Fetching historical OHLCV data via Bitstamp API for backtesting trading strategies
Processing fiat withdrawals via Bitstamp API for automated treasury operations
not_for: DeFi or decentralized exchange trading (Bitstamp is centralized)
Altcoin trading with hundreds of pairs (Bitstamp offers ~70 pairs — use Binance for wide altcoin coverage)
High-frequency trading requiring sub-millisecond latency (Bitstamp is not colocation optimized)
best_when: A regulated institution or professional trader needs a compliant European cryptocurrency exchange with REST and WebSocket APIs, MICA/MiFID2-compliant reporting, and fiat on/off ramps in EUR/GBP/USD.
avoid_when: You need wide altcoin selection, DeFi access, or ultra-high-frequency trading infrastructure.

auth.notes: API key + API secret for HMAC-SHA256 request signing. X-Auth-Signature header with HMAC of nonce + customer ID + API key. X-Auth-Nonce (millisecond timestamp + random). X-Auth-Timestamp. X-Auth-Version: v2. API key with permission bits: Account Balance, Simple Order, All Order, Withdrawal, Full, Cancel. No OAuth2. Public endpoints (market data) require no auth. WebSocket: no auth for public channels; private channels use API key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; trading fees 0.30%-0.50% taker (volume-based discount)
pricing.paid_tiers: Institutional: 0.10%-0.25% maker/taker; Pro accounts: lower fees with volume
pricing.notes: Founded 2011, Luxembourg incorporated. One of the oldest regulated exchanges. EU MiCA compliant (2024). Previously regulated in UK (FCA), EU (Luxembourg Financial Sector Supervisory Commission). Acquired by NXMH (Belgian investment group) 2018. ~70 trading pairs. Fiat: EUR, USD, GBP, CHF. Known for reliability and regulatory compliance. 4M+ customers. Volume-based fee tiers (Tranche 0: 0.5% down to Tranche 8: 0.1% at $20M/month). REST API v2 base: www.bitstamp.net/api/v2/. No official SDK — community libs exist. WebSocket v3 for streaming.

performance.rate_limits: 8,000 requests per 10 minutes (REST); WebSocket: no documented limit

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HMAC-SHA256 signing. X-Auth-Nonce + X-Auth-Timestamp required. MiCA compliant. Luxembourg-regulated. 8,000 req/10min rate limit. WebSocket for streaming. No official SDK. Public endpoints no auth.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order creation is not idempotent — retries may create duplicate orders. Use order IDs returned from creation for status checks. Cancel operations idempotent by order ID.
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC signing: X-Auth-Signature = HMAC-SHA256(nonce + customer_id + api_key, api_secret)
X-Auth-Nonce: concatenate Unix timestamp milliseconds + random string (not just timestamp)
Rate limit: 8,000 requests per 10 minutes — burst is allowed, sustained trading needs care
Order creation not idempotent — check open orders before placing to avoid duplicates
Market order vs limit order: market orders execute immediately at best available price
KYC/AML required for fiat withdrawals — API-level withdrawals require verified account

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 signing with nonce prevents replay attacks. API key permission bits (granular). MiCA + GDPR + AML5 compliance. Luxembourg CSSF regulated. Cryptocurrency financial data — high security target. EU data residency. No OAuth2. Nonce-based replay protection. KYC/AML for fiat operations.
